# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1106

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1106 — 2026-05-12T17:56:00Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED

## Context
- Manifest: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1106/.pan/review/agent-pan-1106-review-bf1eb8ac/context.json
- Branch: feature/1106
- Workspace: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1106

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Status | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- |
| security | done | 0 |
| correctness | done | 0 |
| performance | done | 0 |
| requirements | done | 1 |

## Blocking Findings

### [requirements] Ship/test rows do not expose an attachable tmux session for idle live sessions — `src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:219`
The requirements reviewer found that the new ship/test nodes are visible, but they do not populate `tmuxSession` because that field is only set for `work` and `planning` session types. The frontend computes the terminal target as `session.tmuxSession || (session.presence === 'active' ? session.sessionId : undefined)` in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/SessionView/SessionPanel.tsx:118`, while live tmux sessions can be reported as `idle` or `suspended` by `deriveSessionPresence()`. As a result, a live idle ship/test row can render “No terminal session available” instead of attaching to the session, leaving the acceptance criteria “Where to click to attach to the right session” and “Clicking a role row opens the right tmux session / conversation” partially unmet.

Suggested fix from the reviewer: populate `tmuxSession: checkId` for ship/test nodes when the tmux session exists, or make the session panel fallback to `sessionId` for any non-ended session type whose `sessionId` is the canonical tmux name.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [requirements] Per-role cost display remains ambiguous
The requirements reviewer noted that `SessionNode` includes model, status, duration, presence, and round metadata, but no per-node cost field (`packages/contracts/src/types.ts:445`). If “where applicable” means every role row should show cost inline, the contract needs a new field; if issue-level/review-level cost UI already satisfies it, no change is required.

## Clean Sub-roles
- security
- correctness
- performance

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1106/.pan/review/agent-pan-1106-review-bf1eb8ac/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1106 -m "Fixed review issues"`
